deep-iris.$description: Deep Iris — Page canvas, hero background, primary surface — the brand-defining midnight violet that sets the entire dark mode identity

iris-shadow.$description: Iris Shadow — Elevated card surfaces on dark canvas, secondary card backgrounds — one step lighter than canvas for depth without breaking the violet atmosphere

iris-glow.$description: Iris Glow — Mid-tone accent surface, footer background, highlighted metric blocks — mid-violet for tertiary elevation and accent fills

iris-pulse.$description: Iris Pulse — Violet action color for filled buttons, selected navigation states, and focused conversion moments.

iris-border.$description: Iris Border — Card border outlines, subtle surface edges on dark mode — keeps card perimeters defined without breaking the violet mood

iris-veil.$description: Iris Veil — Body and card border accent, secondary surface outline — lighter violet for hairline separators on dark surfaces

lilac-mist.$description: Lilac Mist — Line-art illustration stroke, decorative SVG fills, wireframe graphics — soft violet for technical/medical illustration overlay

clinical-cyan.$description: Clinical Cyan — Data highlights, chart strokes, icon accents, inline links, interactive borders — the primary data-viz and link color across the UI

cyan-soft.$description: Cyan Soft — Secondary cyan accent, softer data labels, gradient endpoints paired with Clinical Cyan

mint-vital.$description: Mint Vital — Green outline accent for tags, dividers, and focused UI edges. Use as a supporting accent, not as a status color

teal-signal.$description: Teal Signal — Highlight accent for key data callouts and chart emphasis — brighter teal-cyan for moments that need to pop

cloud-white.$description: Cloud White — Primary text, button text on dark, button backgrounds in light section, high-contrast headings

pearl.$description: Pearl — Soft icon strokes, subtle dividers, and low-emphasis decorative details. Do not promote it to the primary CTA color

ash.$description: Ash — Muted text on light sections, secondary borders, low-emphasis labels

fog.$description: Fog — Hairline dividers, disabled borders, low-contrast separators

gilroy.$description: Sole typeface across the entire system — weight 500 for body, links, buttons, cards; weight 600 for all headings and display. Gilroy's geometric humanism gives medical data a friendly, non-clinical warmth

deep-iris-canvas.$description: Surface level 1: Page and hero background — the foundational dark surface

iris-shadow.$description: Surface level 2: Elevated cards and panels on dark canvas

iris-glow.$description: Surface level 3: Highlighted metric blocks, footer, mid-elevation panels

pearl-inversion.$description: Surface level 4: Light-section backgrounds, inverted content blocks

cloud-white.$description: Surface level 5: Top-elevation card surfaces in light sections, button fills
